Memory Leak
-----------
* Double check performance of ReverseComplement, specifically the

First issue: looks like there is a memory leak? With longer input, it's using
up all the memory on my computer?

Yeah. It's definitely leaking memory. Even when I break up the input into
smaller chunks. What's up with that?

I think the problem is we use recursion to get to the end of the current
sequence, thus having it allocated on the stack, but rather than return before
doing the next sequence, we continue to do recursion from there. So everything
ends up on the stack. Interesting.

Anyway, I should process one sequence at a time in the call and have a higher
level loop to go over multiple sequences.

Technically the format is:
> (single line of header text)
(lines of sequence)
> (...)
(...)
...

The tricky part will be detecting the end of the sequence.

Wait a second. Isn't this tail recursive? So why are we still leaking memory
here?

Hmm... This certainly seems like a bug. I could work around it. But I should
figure out what the bug is. Somehow.

How to figure out what the leak is?

Are we not doing tail calls properly? Is GC falling behind perhaps?

Let me try tuning GC to be as aggressive as possible? I don't know how it
could fall behind though. Maybe it keeps getting interrupted?

Looks like it's constantly getting interrupted and so is never able to make
meaningful progress towards garbage collection.

Here we have:
* A: An object is GC allocated.
* F: An object was freed.
* I: GC was interrupted during pop frame or compact, so the object is moved to
  unmarked.
* M: An object was marked.
* U: Anything left unmarked is unreachable.
* r: Saved to marked on pop frame.
* R: Saved to marked on compact frame.
* S: Resurrection of something saved due to interrupted GC.
* newline: Set up the next GC.

Some things that are clear:
* We are doing lots of GC allocations. It's not that everything is on the
  stack.
* We are not falling behind on frees. We easily free everything the GC thinks
  is free.
* We are not freeing nearly enough.

Why is compact frame not freeing the strings from the previous iteration?

I feel like if we add another function call any time we switch to a new
sequence, the memory leak would go away.

No. That didn't help at all. Hmm...

No. Something is very weird here. We interrupt GC. When that happens, we move
a whole slew of objects over to 'unmarked'. We finish the GC. We don't save
any of those objects. So why aren't they all then 'free' and reclaimed right
away?
